steps.title: Write price from quantity
steps.content:
Let $n$ be the number of products sold. The price starts at $96$ dollars and decreases by $1.50$ for each product sold, so
$$
\text{price}=96-1.5n
$$

steps.title: Set the revenue equation
steps.content:
Revenue is price times quantity:
$$
n(96-1.5n)=1{,}522.50
$$
Rearrange and divide by $1.5$:
$$
n^2-64n+1015=0
$$

steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content:
Use the quadratic formula:
$$
n=\frac{64\pm\sqrt{64^2-4(1015)}}{2}=\frac{64\pm 6}{2}
$$
So $n=35$ or $n=29$.

steps.title: Choose the listed value
steps.content: Of the two possible values, only $29$ is listed in the choices. The answer is A.
